name: Nuada Airgetlam, later Lugh Lamfada
shortDescription: Nuada was the first king of the Tuatha Dé Danann. After losing his hand in battle, he was replaced by Lugh Lamfada until a silver hand was crafted for him.
description: Nuada Airgetlám, meaning "Nuada of the Silver Hand," was the first king of the Tuatha Dé Danann, the mythical race who preceded the Gaels in Ireland. He led them in their arrival to Ireland and their battles against the Fir Bolg, culminating in the First Battle of Mag Tuired. During this battle, Nuada lost his hand in combat with Sreng, a Fir Bolg warrior. Due to this physical blemish, he was no longer considered fit to rule, as a king was expected to be physically perfect. He was temporarily replaced by Bres, a half-Fomorian. However, Dian Cécht, the physician of the Tuatha Dé Danann, crafted a silver hand for Nuada, allowing him to reclaim his throne. Later, Miach, Dian Cécht's son, replaced the silver hand with a hand of flesh and blood. Nuada was eventually killed by Balor of the Evil Eye during the Second Battle of Mag Tuired, a battle in which Lugh Lamfada played a critical role in securing victory for the Tuatha Dé Danann. Lugh's rise to prominence was, in part, due to Nuada's earlier reign and the principles of kingship he embodied.
quests: Leading the Tuatha Dé Danann to Ireland
Fighting in the First Battle of Mag Tuired
Reclaiming the throne after receiving a silver hand

sources.title: Lebor na hUidre (Book of the Dun Cow)
